blockingIssues.description: No new content-depth fix feature was added after round 1, so the prior blocker remains unresolved at the current HEAD: the four new content-depth surfaces still import the custom `BetterCard` wrapper for their shell instead of importing shadcn `Card` primitives directly, while `validation-contract.md` explicitly requires 'shadcn Card components (not custom cards)' for `VAL-CONTENT-018`. The regression test was also rewritten to enforce `BetterCard`, so the current suite no longer protects the stricter contract. Earlier HFT Edge / LLM Product review blockers around reciprocal graph links and lazy loading were fixed later in the milestone and are not the active blocker at HEAD.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/content-model.md` to record two factual graph-shell constraints discovered during review: `src/content/graph-nodes.ts` `related` links are directional and must be updated reciprocally for two-way traversal, and new graph surfaces in `src/components/graph/GraphExplorer.tsx` are expected to stay behind lazy-loading boundaries rather than shipping in the initial bundle.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a feature adds graph nodes or graph-backed content surfaces, explicitly require reciprocal updates to existing nodes' `related` arrays and require new surface registrations in `GraphExplorer` to use `dynamic()` / lazy-loading instead of eager imports.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `content-hft-edge-surface` shipped with one-way `hft-edge` relationships plus an eager `HftEdgeSurface` import, and `content-llm-product-surface` repeated the one-way-edge mistake for `llm-product`. Both issues were only corrected later by `content-graph-topology-and-labels` (`src/content/graph-nodes.ts`, `src/components/graph/GraphExplorer.tsx`).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a narrow baseline-repair exception path: if validators expose a small pre-existing failure unrelated to the assigned feature, allow the worker to record the baseline issue, repair it, rerun validators, and mark `followedProcedure=false` rather than forcing an immediate return or ad hoc deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `content-hft-edge-surface` and `content-enrich-existing-surfaces` handoffs reported justified deviations after fixing small pre-existing contract failures before they could leave their assigned work validator-clean.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile the repo-wide guidance for graph-surface card shells. The current content-depth contract says new surfaces must use direct shadcn `Card` components 'not custom cards', while the live design-system docs and production pattern treat `BetterCard` as the approved liquid-metal wrapper around shadcn Card. Pick one rule and align the contract, tests, and worker guidance to it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `validation-contract.md` `VAL-CONTENT-018` forbids custom cards, but `.factory/library/design-system.md` says the liquid-metal styling is preserved through a wrapper around the shadcn Card primitive, and `src/components/graph/surfaces/__tests__/content-surface-card-contract.test.ts` now enforces `BetterCard` imports/usages. Reviewers therefore disagreed about whether the current implementation passes or fails the same requirement.

rejectedObservations.observation: The baseline-validator exception note from `content-enrich-existing-surfaces` should trigger a separate guidance change from the same note raised in `content-hft-edge-surface`.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate; both reviews describe the same procedural gap around small pre-existing validator failures, so one combined guidance recommendation is sufficient.

rejectedObservations.observation: Treat the original `content-hft-edge-surface` and `content-llm-product-surface` review failures as active milestone blockers for the next rerun.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because the current HEAD already includes the reciprocal graph links and dynamic imports added later in `content-graph-topology-and-labels`; those earlier failures are useful historical context but not the remaining blocker that needs a new fix feature.

orchestratorOverride.note: Round-2's blocking failure is stale after artifact-only alignment of VAL-CONTENT-018 and the content-depth feature specs to the canonical BetterCard-wrapper rule. The implementation already matches that contract, test/typecheck/lint/build all pass, and user-testing should proceed against the updated contract.
